teachings.teaching: Christ’s identity and significance are presented through a documented lineage extending from Abraham to David and onward to Jesus.
The text emphasizes continuity of history across major eras (from David to the Babylonian exile, then to Christ), reinforcing a sense of fulfillment across generations.
The repeated structural pattern (three sets of fourteen generations) highlights intentional organization and thematic completeness rather than a random list.

goals.goal: To establish and legitimize Jesus’ connection to Israel’s covenant history by tracing his genealogy from Abraham and David through the period of the Babylonian exile up to Christ.
